PixiJS V5 Tutorial (40) - The Finale: A Year-Long Marathon Wraps Up
This is the closing post for the entire Pixi series, so let's give it a proper ending.
Honestly, I never planned to write about Pixi at all. The main reason I ever touched it was a freelance project.

The project used Pixi to render a particular section of the screen, so to actually get the work done I just had to grit my teeth and learn it. And I'll admit — back then my mindset was purely "just get through this." But somehow that turned into me building a whole game system for the company.
(The image below was converted from a
to a GIF, so the quality is a bit rough.)
So what did this RPG game include?
Fetching new data from the backend
A boss status panel (only viewable after a skill is triggered)
accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture
